programs.title: The King Of Laughter
programs.desc: Naples, 1904: Italian theatre is thriving, and comic actor, Eduardo Scarpetta is the box office king. Known in the Neapolitan theater for his cheeky alter egos, Scarpetta’s larger-than-life stage productions were matched only by his eccentric personal life. Composed of wives, partners, lovers, legitimate and illegitimate children (including a young Eduardo De Filippo, famed Neapolitan playwright), Scarpetta’s home situation resembled one of his comedies more than a traditional family. At the height of his popularity, the comedian risked everything by staging a parody of the great Italian poet, Gabriele D’Annunzio. Booed and hissed from the stage by younger rivals, Scarpetta was sued for plagiarism, leading to the first ever copyright lawsuit in Italy. Despite legal troubles and familial strife, Scarpetta fought not only for his craft but for his legacy as one of the great thespians of Italian theater.

programs.title: History Of The Occult
programs.desc: It's '60 Minutes To midnight's' last broadcast, the most famous journalism show on TV. Tonight's is Adrian Marcato, who might expose a conspiracy that links the Government with an actual Coven.

programs.title: 55 Steps
programs.desc: 55 Steps is based a the inspiring true story of Eleanor Riese, a mental illness patient herself, who brings a class action suit to give competent mental patients the right to have a say in their medication while they're in a hospital, and Colette Hughes, the lawyer appointed to her case.

programs.title: Discovering Film
programs.desc: From Alien and The Elephant Man to Harry Potter, this is a look at the life and hugely successful career of the British actor.

programs.title: The Most Beautiful Day In The World
programs.desc: A theatre entrepreneur has no talent for art or business, but when a distant relative entrusts him with the care of his nephew, the man discovers he has a phenomenal talent that could change the fate of his business.
